fundamentals.name: Datadog, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Datadog, Inc. operates an observability and security platform for cloud applications in the United States and internationally. The company's products comprise infrastructure and application performance monitoring, log management, observability pipelines, synthetics, real user monitoring, product analytics, continuous profiler, database monitoring, data observability, LLM observability, error tracking, network monitoring, incident response, workflow automation and App builder, event management, bits AI SRE, cloud cost management, cloud security, code security, cloud SIEM, threat management, sensitive data scanner, and CI visibility. Datadog, Inc. was incorporated in 2010 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue and earnings expansion, but trades at a premium valuation with thin operating margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates by 18.3%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 32.2% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 113.4% indicate strong top- and bottom-line expansion.
Forward P/E of 79.27, while high, is significantly lower than trailing P/E of 593.74, suggesting expected earnings growth.
Gross margin of 79.9% is very strong, indicating efficient cost of goods sold.
Total cash of $4.76B provides ample liquidity, with free cash flow of $936.73M.
Analyst consensus is 'strong_buy' with a mean target of $223.93.
negatives: Operating margin of 0.8% and profit margin of 3.7% are thin, indicating high operating expenses relative to revenue.
Return on equity of 3.9% and return on assets of -0.2% suggest inefficient use of capital and assets.
Debt/Equity ratio of 32.22 is high, though offset by substantial cash reserves.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 21.87 indicates a very high valuation relative to current sales.

news.paragraph: Datadog is benefiting from the increasing adoption of AI in production environments, according to recent analysis. The company recently reported its first quarter with over $1 billion in revenue and raised its 2026 outlook, signaling strong business momentum. An analyst at BTIG raised their price target to $255, reinforcing a generally positive sentiment around the stock.
